rows.title: Cole and the Snowflake
paragraphs: Cole opened the door. There was the fish! "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi!" said the fish back.
"bonk?" said Cole. "bonk!" said the fish. Then the snowflake wiggled. Just a little. Everyone saw it.

"bonk!" said Cole. The fish said "bonk!" back. Then the snowflake sneezed. The fish said "bless you" to the snowflake. There was a small bounced moment that nobody quite witnessed in full. Everything in the room had picked up a faint brown tint.
Cole waved at the fish. "Bye for now!" said Cole. The fish waved back.

rows.title: Cole and the Squirrel
paragraphs: Cole spotted the squirrel from across the room. Cole waved. The squirrel waved back.
"bonk?" said Cole. "bonk!" said the squirrel. Then the muffin wiggled. Just a little. Everyone saw it.

"bonk!" said Cole. The squirrel said "bonk!" back. Then the muffin sneezed. The squirrel said "bless you" to the muffin. For exactly two seconds, Cole marched like it was a job. Everything in the room had picked up a faint gold tint.
Cole waved at the squirrel. "Bye for now!" said Cole. The squirrel waved back.

rows.title: Cole Sees the Butterfly
paragraphs: Cole ran outside. The frog was right there. "Hi frog!" said Cole.
"blorp?" said Cole. "blorp!" said the frog. Then the butterfly wiggled. Just a little. Everyone saw it.

"blorp!" said Cole. The frog said "blorp!" back. Then the butterfly sneezed. The frog said "bless you" to the butterfly. Cole crawled without thinking. It was a thing they did now. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little pink.
Cole picked up the frog for a hug. "Night, frog," said Cole. Then Cole curled up.

rows.title: Cole and the Butterfly
paragraphs: Cole opened the door. There was the squirrel! "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi!" said the squirrel back.
"gloop?" said Cole. "gloop!" said the squirrel. Then the butterfly wiggled. Just a little. Everyone saw it.

"gloop!" said Cole. The squirrel said "gloop!" back. Then the butterfly sneezed. The squirrel said "bless you" to the butterfly. For exactly two seconds, Cole bounced like it was a job. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly pink.
Cole waved at the squirrel. "Bye for now!" said Cole. The squirrel waved back.

rows.title: Hi, Chick!
paragraphs: Cole opened the door. There was the chick! "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi!" said the chick back.
"blorp?" said Cole. "blorp!" said the chick. Then the bread wiggled. Just a little. Everyone saw it.

"blorp!" said Cole. The chick said "blorp!" back. Then the bread sneezed. The chick said "bless you" to the bread. Cole bounced one more time, just to make a point. A faint orange glow hung over the scene by then.
Cole picked up the chick for a hug. "Night, chick," said Cole. Then Cole curled up.

rows.title: Cole Sees the Star
paragraphs: Cole spotted the fish from across the room. Cole waved. The fish waved back.
"bonk!" said Cole. The fish said "bonk!" back. Then the star sneezed. The fish said "bless you" to the star.

"bonk?" said Cole. "bonk!" said the fish. Then the star wiggled. Just a little. Everyone saw it. Cole tiptoed without thinking. It was a thing they did now. The purple thing was happening again, whatever it was.
Now Cole is sleepy. The fish is sleepy too. Good night, fish. Good night, Cole.

rows.title: Hi, Rocket!
paragraphs: Cole opened the door. There was the bunny! "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi!" said the bunny back.
"spoon hat?" said Cole. "spoon hat!" said the bunny. Then the rocket wiggled. Just a little. Everyone saw it.

"spoon hat!" said Cole. The bunny said "spoon hat!" back. Then the rocket sneezed. The bunny said "bless you" to the rocket. For exactly two seconds, Cole crawled like it was a job. A faint sky blue glow hung over the scene by then.
Now Cole is sleepy. The bunny is sleepy too. Good night, bunny. Good night, Cole.

rows.title: The Rainbow and the Pig
paragraphs: Cole spotted the pig from across the room. Cole waved. The pig waved back.
"tiny piano!" said Cole. The pig said "tiny piano!" back. Then the rainbow sneezed. The pig said "bless you" to the rainbow.

"tiny piano?" said Cole. "tiny piano!" said the pig. Then the rainbow wiggled. Just a little. Everyone saw it. Cole tiptoed without thinking. It was a thing they did now. The brown thing was happening again, whatever it was.
Cole hugged the pig one more time. "Come back tomorrow?" said Cole. The pig nodded.

rows.title: Cole Says Hi!
paragraphs: Cole opened the door. There was the pig! "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi!" said the pig back.
"splat?" said Cole. "splat!" said the pig. Then the peas wiggled. Just a little. Everyone saw it.

"splat!" said Cole. The pig said "splat!" back. Then the peas sneezed. The pig said "bless you" to the peas. Cole bounced without thinking. It was a thing they did now. The purple thing was happening again, whatever it was.
Cole hugged the pig one more time. "Come back tomorrow?" said Cole. The pig nodded.

rows.title: Cole Says Hi!
paragraphs: "Come on, mouse!" said Cole. The mouse came. Cole led the way.
"gloop!" said Cole. The mouse said "gloop!" back. Then the peas sneezed. The mouse said "bless you" to the peas.

"gloop?" said Cole. "gloop!" said the mouse. Then the peas wiggled. Just a little. Everyone saw it. Somewhere in there, Cole slid for emphasis. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little rainbow.
Time for a hug. Cole hugged the mouse. Then Cole yawned. The mouse yawned too.

rows.title: How Cole Found the Bent Spoon
paragraphs: Cole opened a door and the koala tumbled in. "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi back!" said the koala — somehow.
Cole tapped the bent spoon like a button. "fwoosh!" said Cole. "fwoosh!" said the koala. The bent spoon did exactly nothing, which was funny.

"fwoosh," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The bent spoon flipped over by itself. The koala looked impressed. Cole wiggled without thinking. It was a thing they did now. Everything in the room had picked up a faint purple tint.
Cole pulled the koala into a small pillow fort. "We made it," said Cole. The koala yawned.

rows.title: How Cole Found the Pocket-sized Door
paragraphs: Cole packed a tiny bag and called the foal. The foal came running. Cole led the way.
Cole tapped the pocket-sized door like a button. "soggy sock!" said Cole. "soggy sock!" said the foal. The pocket-sized door did exactly nothing, which was funny.

"soggy sock," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The pocket-sized door flipped over by itself. The foal looked impressed. There was a small jumped moment that nobody quite witnessed in full. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little crimson.
By the end of the day, Cole and the foal were tired and happy. They hugged. Then they went to bed.

rows.title: How Cole Found the Suspicious Envelope
paragraphs: Cole told the hedgehog, "Today is going to be a big one." The hedgehog agreed. They were ready.
"glorp," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The suspicious envelope flipped over by itself. The hedgehog looked impressed.

"glorp!" said Cole. The hedgehog said it back. Then they said it together. The suspicious envelope hummed along, kind of. There was a small peeked moment that nobody quite witnessed in full. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little peach.
Cole tucked the hedgehog in first, then climbed in too. "Same time tomorrow?" said Cole. The hedgehog was already half asleep.

rows.title: Cole and the Hallway Pass
paragraphs: Cole told the turtle, "Today is going to be a big one." The turtle agreed. They were ready.
Cole tapped the hallway pass like a button. "wubba!" said Cole. "wubba!" said the turtle. The hallway pass did exactly nothing, which was funny.

"wubba!" said Cole. The turtle said it back. Then they said it together. The hallway pass hummed along, kind of. Cole floated without thinking. It was a thing they did now. A faint green glow hung over the scene by then.
By the end of the day, Cole and the turtle were happy and ready for whatever came next. They high-fived and headed home.

rows.title: Cole and the Sandwich
paragraphs: It was a bright morning. Cole grabbed the piglet and headed out. "We have a plan," said Cole.
Cole tapped the sandwich like a button. "wubba!" said Cole. "wubba!" said the piglet. The sandwich did exactly nothing, which was funny.

"wubba!" said Cole. The piglet said it back. Then they said it together. The sandwich hummed along, kind of. There was a small floated moment that nobody quite witnessed in full. The silver thing was happening again, whatever it was.
By the end of the day, Cole and the piglet were happy and ready for whatever came next. They high-fived and headed home.

rows.title: How Cole Found the Tiny Clipboard
paragraphs: It was a bright morning. Cole grabbed the turtle and headed out. "We have a plan," said Cole.
"mister floof!" said Cole. The turtle said it back. Then they said it together. The tiny clipboard hummed along, kind of.

Cole tapped the tiny clipboard like a button. "mister floof!" said Cole. "mister floof!" said the turtle. The tiny clipboard did exactly nothing, which was funny. For exactly two seconds, Cole danced like it was a job. Everything in the room had picked up a faint pink tint.
By the end of the day, Cole and the turtle were tired and happy. They hugged. Then they went to bed.

rows.title: Cole and the Chick
paragraphs: It was a bright morning. Cole grabbed the chick and headed out. "We have a plan," said Cole.
Cole tapped the cereal box like a button. "mister floof!" said Cole. "mister floof!" said the chick. The cereal box did exactly nothing, which was funny.

"mister floof!" said Cole. The chick said it back. Then they said it together. The cereal box hummed along, kind of. For exactly two seconds, Cole bounced like it was a job. The purple thing was happening again, whatever it was.
Cole grabbed the chick's paw. "Onto the next thing," said Cole. They walked home together.

rows.title: The Dramatic Cape Adventure
paragraphs: Cole opened a door and the hedgehog tumbled in. "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi back!" said the hedgehog — somehow.
"sneezy-pants!" said Cole. The hedgehog said it back. Then they said it together. The dramatic cape hummed along, kind of.

"sneezy-pants," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The dramatic cape flipped over by itself. The hedgehog looked impressed. Cole zoomed without thinking. It was a thing they did now. A faint cherry red glow hung over the scene by then.
By the end of the day, Cole and the hedgehog were tired and happy. They hugged. Then they went to bed.

rows.title: Cole and the Piglet
paragraphs: Cole spotted the piglet across the yard. "Hey piglet!" said Cole. The piglet was already smiling.
Cole tapped the shopping list like a button. "stinky bananas!" said Cole. "stinky bananas!" said the piglet. The shopping list did exactly nothing, which was funny.

"stinky bananas," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The shopping list flipped over by itself. The piglet looked impressed. Cole danced one more time, just to make a point. Everything in the room had picked up a faint silver tint.
By the end of the day, Cole and the piglet were tired and happy. They hugged. Then they went to bed.

rows.title: Cole and the Turtle
paragraphs: Cole spotted the turtle across the yard. "Hey turtle!" said Cole. The turtle was already smiling.
Cole tapped the sleepy megaphone like a button. "tiny piano!" said Cole. "tiny piano!" said the turtle. The sleepy megaphone did exactly nothing, which was funny.

"tiny piano!" said Cole. The turtle said it back. Then they said it together. The sleepy megaphone hummed along, kind of. For exactly two seconds, Cole skidded like it was a job. The teal thing was happening again, whatever it was.
By the end of the day, Cole and the turtle were tired and happy. They hugged. Then they went to bed.

rows.title: Cole and the Grilled Cheese Mystery
paragraphs: Cole and the duckling were at the blanket fort. Cole had been saving the grilled cheese all morning. The duckling was extra excited.
The grilled cheese had vanished. Cole felt secretly proud about it, in a way that meant business. The stone giant noticed and tried to act normal. It was not working.

The duckling suddenly found something fascinating on the ceiling. "Hey," said Cole. The duckling would not look down. The grilled cheese had vanished. So had the duckling's alibi. Connected. The rainbow thing was happening again, whatever it was.

The duckling hiccuped one more time and a tiny crumb of grilled cheese popped out. Cole laughed so hard. "bubble wand!" yelled Cole. The duckling echoed back, mouth full. For exactly two seconds, Cole tiptoed like it was a job. A distant "mystery jelly" echoed from somewhere, possibly a memory.
That night, Cole curled up. The duckling, full of stolen crumbs, curled up too. Tomorrow: more snacks. Tonight: rest.

rows.title: How Cole Tried to Fix the Broken Contraption
paragraphs: It started at the garden. Cole looked at the dragon and made up their mind: today they would fix the broken contraption. No matter what.
The yeti appeared out of nowhere. "You? Today? Trying to fix the broken contraption?" said the yeti. "Absolutely not." It folded its arms in a way that meant business.
Cole walked up to the yeti in full secretly proud mode. Not asking permission. Not apologizing. Just secretly proud. The yeti had not prepared for secretly proud. The yeti took a small step back.

"crinkle-bonk!" said Cole. The yeti heard it, made a small noise, and stepped aside in a way that looked rehearsed. Cole fixed the broken contraption. The dragon applauded the yeti, because manners. Somewhere in there, Cole leapt for emphasis. Everything in the room had picked up a faint sunset orange tint. Later someone would swear they heard "squonk" in the rafters. Somebody had brought ramen. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
Back home, Cole replayed it in their head: how the dragon had been right there, how it had all worked out. The dragon curled up. Tomorrow could be just as good.

rows.title: Cole's Big Show
paragraphs: There was going to be a show. Cole had been planning it for days. The stage was the playground. The co-star was the goldfish. The crucial prop was a rubber chicken.
Then everything went wrong. The rubber chicken broke. Actually broke. In half. Cole froze. The goldfish froze. The pillows watched.

Cole took a breath, spun forward, and shouted whatever came to mind: "noodle scarf!" The audience leaned in. Sometimes nonsense lands. And one of them, very quietly, said "fluffypants." some cheese puffs sat off to the side, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day.

"noodle scarf!" yelled Cole, pointing at the broken rubber chicken. The rubber chicken twitched. Twitched again. Then it did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. Everything in the room had picked up a faint electric blue tint. Throughout, Cole stayed snacky. Steadily snacky. A hallway ghost watched the whole thing happen and offered no comment.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the goldfish. The show would never be performed exactly that way again. That was kind of the point.

rows.title: The Day Cole Tried to Make the Grumpiest Thing Giggle
paragraphs: At the nurse's office, Cole told the koala the plan. The plan was simple: make the grumpiest thing giggle. The koala nodded immediately. It was on.
There was just one problem: a talking horse stood between Cole and the goal. "Nope," said the talking horse. "Not today. Not make the grumpiest thing giggle. Not on my watch."

And then Cole did the most lemon yellow thing imaginable: walked right past the talking horse while pretending to be a lemon yellow traffic cone. The talking horse watched, confused. The plan was working. Cole moonwalked one more time, just to make a point. And one of them, very quietly, said "pickle juice."

"honk!" said Cole. The talking horse heard it, made a small noise, and stepped aside in a way that looked rehearsed. Cole made the grumpiest thing giggle. The koala applauded the talking horse, because manners. Throughout, Cole stayed confused. Steadily confused. Somebody had brought fried chicken. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
On the way home, Cole replayed the moment everything turned around. The koala kept bouncing along, already ready for whatever was next. Win logged. Onward.

rows.title: The Banana Phone Broke But Cole Did Not
paragraphs: At the tiny city, Cole set everything up. The eagle practiced its part. The banana phone sat front and center. The whole show kind of depended on the banana phone working.
The banana phone just... gave up. It sat there. Completely uncooperative. The eagle poked it. Nothing. The eagle poked it again. Still nothing. Cole had eight seconds to fix this.

So Cole improvised. "flumpy!" Cole yelled. Then slid. Then yelled "flumpy!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT now. Everything in the room had picked up a faint tomato red tint. Just then a "jiggly blorp" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it. Cole kept feeling grumpy about the whole thing.

"flumpy!" yelled Cole, pointing at the broken banana phone. The banana phone twitched. Twitched again. Then it did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. Somebody had brought cereal. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded. Off to the side, the talking sandwich was processing all of this with visible difficulty.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the eagle. The show would never be performed exactly that way again. That was kind of the point.

rows.title: The Loophole at the Meadow
paragraphs: It was a normal day at the meadow. Cole and the eagle were minding their own business. The day was, technically, fine. So far.
The ghost declared the cupcakes forbidden. Cole felt super happy about this development. Possibly more super happy than the ghost had bargained for.

Cole zigzagged sideways while holding the banana phone. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back. The lemon yellow thing was happening again, whatever it was.
Cole held the cupcakes up like a trophy. The ghost could only watch. "blorp!" yelled Cole. Both things were now true at once. Out of nowhere, Cole said "bonkledink" like a small spell.
Heading home, Cole explained the loophole to the eagle one more time. The eagle was nodding seriously, clearly planning to deploy it later. Knowledge: transferred.

rows.title: Cole and the Otter Try to Cross the Wobbly Bridge
paragraphs: It started at the bus. Cole looked at the otter and made up their mind: today they would cross the wobbly bridge. No matter what.
The tiny king stood between Cole and victory. Cole felt curious about it. The tiny king did not look like it was going to move on its own.

Cole walked up to the tiny king in full curious mode. Not asking permission. Not apologizing. Just curious. The tiny king had not prepared for curious. The tiny king took a small step back. Somewhere in there, Cole tumbled for emphasis. Later someone would swear they heard "mystery jelly" in the rafters.

"honk!" said Cole. The tiny king heard it, made a small noise, and stepped aside in a way that looked rehearsed. Cole crossed the wobbly bridge. The otter applauded the tiny king, because manners. The golden thing was happening again, whatever it was. some dumplings sat off to the side, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day.
On the way home, Cole replayed the moment everything turned around. The otter kept bouncing along, already ready for whatever was next. Win logged. Onward.

rows.title: Cole and the Troll's Rule
paragraphs: It was a normal day at the bathtub. Cole and the tiger were minding their own business. The day was, technically, fine. So far.
The troll declared the tacos forbidden. Cole felt overexcited about this development. Possibly more overexcited than the troll had bargained for.

Cole spun sideways while holding the map covered in crumbs. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back. A faint mint green glow hung over the scene by then. A "bubble wand" slipped out before Cole could stop it.
Cole held up the map covered in crumbs and said "splat." The map covered in crumbs did something briefly impressive that nobody could later describe. The tacos arrived in Cole's hand.
The tiger watched the loophole work and looked impressed. The tiger was going to try this later. Cole had taught the tiger something brand new. Bedtime: earned.

rows.title: Cole and the Fire Bird's Rule
paragraphs: It was a normal day at the park. Cole and the parrot were minding their own business. The day was, technically, fine. So far.
The fire bird declared the milkshake forbidden. Cole felt secretly proud about this development. Possibly more secretly proud than the fire bird had bargained for.
Cole marched sideways while holding the shopping cart. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back.

"sneezy sandwich!" said Cole. The rule developed a small visible crack. Nobody could see it, but everyone agreed it was there. The fire bird handed over the milkshake without comment. The moss green thing was happening again, whatever it was. And one of them, very quietly, said "zoinks."
The parrot watched the loophole work and looked impressed. The parrot was going to try this later. Cole had taught the parrot something brand new. Bedtime: earned.

rows.title: The Wind-up Toy Broke But Cole Did Not
paragraphs: At the grocery aisle, Cole set everything up. The llama practiced its part. The wind-up toy sat front and center. The whole show kind of depended on the wind-up toy working.
Three seconds in, the llama forgot the next part. Just blanked. Then the wind-up toy fell off the table. Cole was on their own.

So Cole improvised. "tiny piano!" Cole yelled. Then tumbled. Then yelled "tiny piano!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT now. Just then a "pickle juice" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it. Throughout, Cole stayed sleepy. Steadily sleepy. Somebody had brought pizza. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.

"tiny piano!" yelled Cole, pointing at the broken wind-up toy. The wind-up toy twitched. Twitched again. Then it did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. Everything in the room had picked up a faint mint green tint. Off to the side, the tiny king was processing all of this with visible difficulty.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the llama. The show would never be performed exactly that way again. That was kind of the point.

rows.title: The Day Cole Saved the Show
paragraphs: There was going to be a show. Cole had been planning it for days. The stage was the playground. The co-star was the theatrical moth. The crucial prop was a mascot head.
Then everything went wrong. The mascot head broke. Actually broke. In half. Cole froze. The theatrical moth froze. The pillows watched.

So Cole improvised. "kabloom!" Cole yelled. Then tiptoed cautiously. Then yelled "kabloom!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT now. Later someone would swear they heard "noodle scarf" in the rafters. Throughout, Cole stayed thoughtfully menacing. Steadily thoughtfully menacing. a bowl of legendary soup sat off to the side, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day.
The tiny judge heckled. Genuinely heckled. The theatrical moth hissed back. Cole kept performing. Conflict made the bit better. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little suspiciously beige.
"kabloom!" yelled Cole, pointing at the broken mascot head. The mascot head twitched. Twitched again. Then it did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the theatrical moth. The show would never be performed exactly that way again. That was kind of the point.

rows.title: How Cole Outsmarted the Indignant Mushroom
paragraphs: Cole and the suspicious duck had arrived at the bakery with a simple plan: have a regular afternoon. The suspicious duck was good at regular afternoons.

The indignant mushroom declared the thunder pancakes forbidden. Cole felt professionally confused about this development. Possibly more professionally confused than the indignant mushroom had bargained for.

Cole waltzed accidentally sideways while holding the apology balloon. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back. The strangely familiar gray thing was happening again, whatever it was.

The indignant mushroom squinted at the apology balloon. The indignant mushroom could not technically object. Cole was technically still inside the rule. The indignant mushroom muttered something and looked away. Just then a "pickle crown" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it.
And just like that, Cole got the thunder pancakes anyway. The indignant mushroom sighed. "rubber duck!" yelled Cole. The rule was still a rule, but Cole had won this round.
Heading home, Cole explained the loophole to the suspicious duck one more time. The suspicious duck was nodding seriously, clearly planning to deploy it later. Knowledge: transferred.

rows.title: The Day the Overconfident Pudding Vanished
paragraphs: At the butterfly house, Cole set the overconfident pudding down for one second. The theatrical moth watched. Cole turned to grab a napkin. Just one second.
The overconfident pudding had vanished. Cole turned heroically mediocre in a way the sparkly squid could feel from across the room. The sparkly squid coughed for no reason.

Cole put on their most heroically mediocre expression and walked very slowly toward the sparkly squid. The sparkly squid tried to back away. "Tell me what you saw," said Cole, staying heroically mediocre. The sparkly squid cracked immediately. Cole marched stubbornly one more time, just to make a point. Everything in the room had picked up a faint forgotten gray tint. And one of them, very quietly, said "splat."
The trail led to the theatrical moth. Of course. The theatrical moth had a single crumb of overconfident pudding on its face. "YOU?" said Cole. The theatrical moth looked away politely.
"boing!" went Cole. "boing!" went the theatrical moth. "boing!" went the overconfident pudding, somehow. The investigation closed on the spot.
That night, Cole curled up. The theatrical moth, full of stolen crumbs, curled up too. Tomorrow: more snacks. Tonight: rest.

rows.title: How Cole Outsmarted the Confused Dragon
paragraphs: It was a normal day at the forest path. Cole and the dramatic lizard were minding their own business. The day was, technically, fine. So far.

The confused dragon declared the official pie forbidden. Cole felt surprisingly enthusiastic about this development. Possibly more surprisingly enthusiastic than the confused dragon had bargained for.

Cole skipped solemnly sideways while holding the pickle jar. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly permission slip blue. Later someone would swear they heard "Captain Noodle" in the rafters.

The confused dragon tried to amend the rule on the fly. The confused dragon said, "Actually—" Cole held the pickle jar higher. The confused dragon could not actually argue against that. The amendment was abandoned.

"mystery jelly!" went Cole. The confused dragon heard a code word it apparently knew, mumbled "ah, the mystery jelly clause," and waved Cole through with the official pie. There was no mystery jelly clause.
The dramatic lizard watched the loophole work and looked impressed. The dramatic lizard was going to try this later. Cole had taught the dramatic lizard something brand new. Bedtime: earned.

rows.title: Cole and the Overly Formal Otter Take the Stage
paragraphs: At the soccer field, Cole set everything up. The overly formal otter practiced its part. The bus ticket sat front and center. The whole show kind of depended on the bus ticket working.
The bus ticket made a noise it should not have been able to make. Then it tipped sideways. Then it kept tipping. It was still tipping. The audience leaned in. Cole thought fast.

Cole took a breath, posed dramatically forward, and shouted whatever came to mind: "jiggly blorp!" The audience leaned in. Sometimes nonsense lands. Everything in the room had picked up a faint obviously red tint.

The indignant mushroom heckled. Genuinely heckled. The overly formal otter hissed back. Cole kept performing. Conflict made the bit better. Just then a "boing" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it. Cole kept feeling gloriously incorrect about the whole thing.

"jiggly blorp!" yelled Cole, pointing at the broken bus ticket. The bus ticket twitched. Twitched again. Then it did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. Somebody had brought mystery smoothie. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the overly formal otter. The show would never be performed exactly that way again. That was kind of the point.

rows.title: The Wind-up Toy Broke But Cole Did Not
paragraphs: At the ice cream truck, Cole set everything up. The suspicious seagull practiced its part. The wind-up toy sat front and center. The whole show kind of depended on the wind-up toy working.
Three seconds in, the suspicious seagull forgot the next part. Just blanked. Then the wind-up toy fell off the table. Cole was on their own.

Cole took a breath, waltzed accidentally forward, and shouted whatever came to mind: "fwoosh!" The audience leaned in. Sometimes nonsense lands. Just then a "crinkle-bonk" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it. Somebody had brought suspicious fruit salad. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
The suspicious accountant heckled. Genuinely heckled. The suspicious seagull hissed back. Cole kept performing. Conflict made the bit better.

"fwoosh!" yelled Cole, pointing at the broken wind-up toy. The wind-up toy twitched. Twitched again. Then it did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. Everything in the room had picked up a faint lunchbox yellow tint. Throughout, Cole stayed mildly legendary. Steadily mildly legendary.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the suspicious seagull. The show was unrepeatable, which felt right. Time to pack up and find the next thing.

rows.title: Cole and the Courtroom Duck's Rule
paragraphs: It was a normal day at the cloud observatory. Cole and the mischievous fox were minding their own business. The day was, technically, fine. So far.
The courtroom duck declared the legendary soup forbidden. Cole felt gravely unimpressed about this development. Possibly more gravely unimpressed than the courtroom duck had bargained for.

Cole spun ceremoniously sideways while holding the wobbly telescope. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little gym sock white.

The courtroom duck squinted at the wobbly telescope. The courtroom duck could not technically object. Cole was technically still inside the rule. The courtroom duck muttered something and looked away.

"stinky bananas!" said Cole. The rule developed a small visible crack. Nobody could see it, but everyone agreed it was there. The courtroom duck handed over the legendary soup without comment. Just then a "burp bubble" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it.
Heading home, Cole explained the loophole to the mischievous fox one more time. The mischievous fox was nodding seriously, clearly planning to deploy it later. Knowledge: transferred.

rows.title: Cole vs the Rule
paragraphs: Cole and the tiny horse had arrived at the classroom with a simple plan: have a regular afternoon. The tiny horse was good at regular afternoons.

The indignant mushroom declared the mystery smoothie forbidden. Cole felt gloriously incorrect about this development. Possibly more gloriously incorrect than the indignant mushroom had bargained for.

Cole spun ceremoniously sideways while holding the mystery coupon. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly strangely familiar gray. A "honk" hung in the air for a second longer than expected.

The indignant mushroom tried to amend the rule on the fly. The indignant mushroom said, "Actually—" Cole held the mystery coupon higher. The indignant mushroom could not actually argue against that. The amendment was abandoned.
And just like that, Cole got the mystery smoothie anyway. The indignant mushroom sighed. "pickle juice!" yelled Cole. The rule was still a rule, but Cole had won this round.
The tiny horse watched the loophole work and looked impressed. The tiny horse was going to try this later. Cole had taught the tiny horse something brand new. Bedtime: earned.

rows.title: Cole and the Mysterious Leftovers Mystery
paragraphs: At the school bus line, Cole set the mysterious leftovers down for one second. The imperious corgi watched. Cole turned to grab a napkin. Just one second.
The mysterious leftovers had vanished. Cole got mysteriously calm about it. The deeply committed scarecrow tried a slow shrug. The shrug arrived late and crooked.
Cole spotted it: a tiny recess orange crumb on the floor. Then another. Then another. The crumbs were leading somewhere. They were not leading to the deeply committed scarecrow.

Then the imperious corgi burped. A specific burp. The kind of burp that smells exactly like mysterious leftovers. "Oh," said Cole. The imperious corgi tried to look surprised. It was not convincing. Cole marched stubbornly one more time, just to make a point.

"soggy genius!" yelled Cole. The imperious corgi squawked back. Everyone agreed to share the next batch of mysterious leftovers. The deeply committed scarecrow demanded a formal apology. Cole gave one. "grumblepoof," went somebody. Nobody asked who.
That night, Cole curled up. The imperious corgi, full of stolen crumbs, curled up too. Tomorrow: more snacks. Tonight: rest.

rows.title: Cole vs the Melodramatic Ghost
paragraphs: It started at the snack bar. Cole looked at the imperious corgi and made up their mind: today they would rescue a stuck friend. No matter what.
But the melodramatic ghost was in the way. The melodramatic ghost did not want Cole to rescue a stuck friend. The melodramatic ghost had its own ideas, and the ideas were terrible.

And then Cole did the most recess orange thing imaginable: walked right past the melodramatic ghost while pretending to be a recess orange traffic cone. The melodramatic ghost watched, confused. The plan was working. Throughout, Cole stayed heroically mediocre. Steadily heroically mediocre.

The melodramatic ghost doubled down. "overconfident pudding or nothing," it said, which barely made sense. Cole decided "or nothing" was not on the table. For exactly two seconds, Cole marched stubbornly like it was a job.

"snorble-doo!" said Cole. The melodramatic ghost heard it, made a small noise, and stepped aside in a way that looked rehearsed. Cole rescued a stuck friend. The imperious corgi applauded the melodramatic ghost, because manners. Just then a "sneezy-pants" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it.
Back home, Cole replayed it in their head: how the imperious corgi had been right there, how it had all worked out. The imperious corgi curled up. Tomorrow could be just as good.

rows.title: The Day the Hot Sauce Vanished
paragraphs: At the diner, Cole had committed to one thing: the hot sauce. The sleepy croc was technically the witness. The mission, technically, involved the hot sauce.
No hot sauce anywhere. Cole went aggressively normal like a tiny detective. The feral librarian hummed a song nobody had asked for.

Cole speed-ran past the feral librarian in a way that read as deliberate. It was not deliberate. It happened to be exactly right. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly parking lot gray. Out of nowhere, Cole said "banana phone" like a small spell. Just then a "noodle scarf" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it.

The sleepy croc was avoiding eye contact in a way that was, frankly, suspicious. Also there were hot sauce crumbs on the floor in a perfect trail leading to the sleepy croc. Cole connected the dots without saying a word.
In the end the hot sauce got shared. Sort of. The sleepy croc got the biggest piece, which felt correct, given the circumstances. Cole did not press charges.
Walking back, Cole replayed the whole thing. The sleepy croc kept pretending nothing happened. Both knew. Neither said. Vibe correct.

rows.title: Cole and the Overconfident Mascot's Rule
paragraphs: Cole was at the bathtub, doing nothing in particular. The axolotl was nearby, also doing nothing. Things were going fine. They would not stay fine.

The overconfident mascot declared the gas station nachos forbidden. Cole felt professionally unhinged about this development. Possibly more professionally unhinged than the overconfident mascot had bargained for.

Cole located the loophole. It involved the lunch tray and a very specific reading of the rule. Cole did not point this out. Pointing it out is rookie behavior. For exactly two seconds, Cole reluctantly arrived like it was a job.

The overconfident mascot watched Cole hold the lunch tray and felt outmaneuvered by precisely one inch of vocabulary. Bureaucracy is just words. Cole had the right ones. Everything in the room had picked up a faint deep purple tint. Just then a "noodle-jumper" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it.

The loophole held. The overconfident mascot could not really stop Cole. Cole walked off with the gas station nachos without technically breaking anything. Quietly satisfying. Somewhere down the hall a tiny "noodle scarf" happened.
On the walk home, Cole mentally filed the loophole for future use. The axolotl was definitely going to use it on Cole eventually. That was fair.

rows.title: Cole vs the Rule
paragraphs: At the cafeteria, Cole was doing the bare minimum. So was the judgmental duck. Suspiciously calm energy. Things were technically fine. They would not stay fine.
The stressed barista declared the leftover pasta forbidden. Cole felt emotionally buffering about this development. Possibly more emotionally buffering than the stressed barista had bargained for.

Cole located the loophole within seconds. It involved the tiny clipboard and a generous interpretation. Pointing it out would be amateur. Cole simply executed. A distant "soggy sock" echoed from somewhere, possibly a memory. Later someone would swear they heard "bonkledink" in the rafters.

The stressed barista watched Cole hold the tiny clipboard and felt outmaneuvered by precisely one inch of vocabulary. Bureaucracy is just words. Cole had the right ones. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little gas station green.

Cole walked off with the leftover pasta. The stressed barista could not technically argue. Cole filed it under: small wins, large vibes. Justice was unevenly served. As expected. Cole gracefully bailed without thinking. It was a thing they did now.
On the walk home, Cole mentally filed the loophole for future use. The judgmental duck was definitely going to use it on Cole eventually. That was fair.

rows.title: The Day Cole Tried to Find the Hidden Treasure
paragraphs: At the nurse's office, Cole had decided. Today: find the hidden treasure. The dramatic goldfish was on board. Quietly. Without making it a thing.
The stressed barista stood between Cole and victory. Cole felt minorly iconic about it. The stressed barista did not look like it was going to move on its own.
Cole speed-ran in a way that read as professionally unhinged. The stressed barista had not budgeted for this. Cole did not break eye contact.

The stressed barista tried something with the cafeteria fries. Cole mentally screenshotted it. Could be evidence later. Probably wouldn't be. A distant "underpants helmet" echoed from somewhere, possibly a memory.

Cole find the hidden treasure. The stressed barista said nothing. Win logged. No comment from the dramatic goldfish, because the dramatic goldfish was already moving on. Everything in the room had picked up a faint late bus yellow tint. And one of them, very quietly, said "honk."
In bed that night, Cole thought about how it had gone. The dramatic goldfish was a co-conspirator now. Quietly proud. Not posting about it. Some wins are just for you.

Cole nodded knowingly in a way that read as professionally unhinged. The feral librarian had not budgeted for this. Cole did not break eye contact. Just then a "underpants helmet" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it.

The feral librarian tried something with the vending machine pretzels. Cole mentally screenshotted it. Could be evidence later. Probably wouldn't be. Somewhere down the hall a tiny "sneezy sandwich" happened.

Cole win the official tiny trophy. The feral librarian said nothing. Win logged. No comment from the hamster, because the hamster was already moving on. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little moss green.
Walking home, Cole thought about how it had gone. The hamster was a co-conspirator now. Quietly proud. Not posting about it. Some wins are just for you.

Cole located the loophole within seconds. It involved the bent spoon and a generous interpretation. Pointing it out would be amateur. Cole simply executed. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little void black. A "moon spoon" slipped out before Cole could stop it.

The very confident rat watched Cole hold the bent spoon and felt outmaneuvered by precisely one inch of vocabulary. Bureaucracy is just words. Cole had the right ones. Somewhere in there, Cole chaotically bolted for emphasis. Just then a "pickle crown" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it.
Cole walked off with the third coffee. The very confident rat could not technically argue. Cole filed it under: small wins, large vibes. Justice was unevenly served. As expected.
On the walk home, Cole mentally filed the loophole for future use. The judgmental duck was definitely going to use it on Cole eventually. That was fair.

rows.title: Cole and the Vending Machine Pretzels Mystery
paragraphs: At the snack bar, Cole had committed to one thing: the vending machine pretzels. The rat was technically the witness. The mission, technically, involved the vending machine pretzels.
The vending machine pretzels had vanished. Cole turned catastrophically fine in a way the vending machine oracle could feel from across the room. The vending machine oracle coughed for no reason.
Cole took a mental screenshot of the room. Three angles. Multiple suspects. The rat looked away too quickly. Filed for later.

The rat was avoiding eye contact in a way that was, frankly, suspicious. Also there were vending machine pretzels crumbs on the floor in a perfect trail leading to the rat. Cole connected the dots without saying a word. A "fluffypants" slipped out before Cole could stop it.

In the end the vending machine pretzels got shared. Sort of. The rat got the biggest piece, which felt correct, given the circumstances. Cole did not press charges. There was a small awkwardly hovered moment that nobody quite witnessed in full. A faint midnight blue glow hung over the scene by then. And one of them, very quietly, said "crusty pretzel."
Walking back, Cole replayed the whole thing. The rat kept pretending nothing happened. Both knew. Neither said. Vibe correct.

The group chat ghost tried something with the energy drink. Cole mentally screenshotted it. Could be evidence later. Probably wouldn't be.

Walking home, Cole thought about how it had gone. The raccoon was a co-conspirator now. Quietly proud. Not posting about it. Some wins are just for you.

rows.title: Who Took the Gas Station Sushi?
paragraphs: At the tiny city, Cole had committed to one thing: the gas station sushi. The chaotic goose was technically the witness. The mission, technically, involved the gas station sushi.
No gas station sushi anywhere. Cole went emotionally buffering like a tiny detective. The stressed barista hummed a song nobody had asked for.

Cole took a long sip and stared past the stressed barista in a way that read as deliberate. It was not deliberate. It happened to be exactly right. The moss green thing was happening again, whatever it was. Somewhere down the hall a tiny "boing" happened. Later someone would swear they heard "stinky bananas" in the rafters.

The chaotic goose was avoiding eye contact in a way that was, frankly, suspicious. Also there were gas station sushi crumbs on the floor in a perfect trail leading to the chaotic goose. Cole connected the dots without saying a word.
Mystery solved without a single accusation. Cole filed it under: ally-related incidents, low priority. Crumbs were everywhere. So was the chaotic goose.
Walking home, Cole mentally workshopped how to retell this. The chaotic goose, still chewing, deserved to be in the story. As a hero. Definitely as a hero.
